name: aiobotocore (Async AWS SDK for Python)

auth.notes: AWS credentials via env (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY), ~/.aws/credentials, or IAM roles

performance.notes: Async I/O avoids blocking; enables concurrent AWS calls

agent_readiness.notes: Async AWS SDK wrapping botocore. import aiobotocore.session. session = aiobotocore.session.AioSession(). async with session.create_client('s3', region_name='us-east-1') as client: resp = await client.get_object(Bucket='my-bucket', Key='file.txt'). async with session.create_client('dynamodb') as client: await client.put_item(TableName='T', Item={...}). Paginators: paginator = client.get_paginator('list_objects'); async for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket='b'): print(page). S3: upload_file, get_object, list_objects_v2. SQS: send_message, receive_message. DynamoDB: put_item, get_item, query. Compatible with boto3 API but async. Used with aiohttp and FastAPI for non-blocking AWS ops.

gotchas: Must use async context manager — async with client: — not direct client instantiation
No type annotations — limited IDE support
Version pinning with boto3 required — aiobotocore pins botocore version
Async paginators differ slightly from sync boto3 paginators
S3Transfer (multipart) not fully async — use aioboto3 for higher-level S3
best_when: async Python applications (FastAPI, aiohttp) needing AWS service calls
High-concurrency AWS integrations (many parallel S3/SQS/DynamoDB calls)
Event-driven systems processing many AWS events simultaneously
avoid_when: Sync Python code — use boto3 directly
When aioboto3 provides the higher-level resource interface needed
summary: aiobotocore is the async AWS SDK for Python. async with session.create_client('s3') as client: await client.get_object(). Async paginators. S3, DynamoDB, SQS, and all AWS services. Apache-2.0 by aio-libs. No type annotations. Must pin with boto3 versions. Best for FastAPI/aiohttp applications needing non-blocking AWS calls. Use boto3 for sync code; aioboto3 for higher-level resource API.
